name: Amp

form_factor.comment: CLI TUI + IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Zed) + @ampcode/sdk (TypeScript) and amp-sdk (Python).

released_in.comment: Publicly launched May 15, 2025 (no waitlist). Originally built inside Sourcegraph, spun off as Amp Frontier Corporation.

latest_major_update.comment: May 6, 2026 — 'Amp, Rebuilt' (codename Neo). Complete CLI rewrite with new architecture: remote-controllable, plugin-powered, auto-compaction.

rules.comment: Full support via AGENTS.md files (plural). Scanned from cwd, parent dirs, subtrees, $HOME/.config/amp/, /etc/ampcode/. Supports @-mentions, globs, YAML frontmatter.

skills.comment: Full support. Skills are directories with SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter. Locations: .agents/skills/ (project), ~/.config/agents/skills/ (user). Can bundle MCP servers via mcp.json.

hooks.comment: Full support via Plugin API (TypeScript). Events: session.start, agent.start, tool.call, tool.result, agent.end. Plugins can respond with allow/reject/modify/synthesize actions.

mcp_servers.comment: Full support. Add via CLI (amp mcp add) or config. Local (command/args/env) and remote (url/headers) servers. OAuth auto-flow. Skill bundling for lazy loading.

custom_commands.comment: Removed January 29, 2026. Replaced by skills and command palette (Ctrl+O) instead of slash commands.

subagents.comment: Full support. Spawned via Task tool. Built-in specialized subagents: Oracle (GPT-5.4), Librarian (code search), Painter (image gen), Review, Search.

model_selection.comment: Pre-set modes (smart=Claude Opus 4.7, deep=GPT-5.5 reasoning, rush=GPT-5.5 no-reasoning, free=mixed). You choose the mode, not the exact model.

approval_mode.comment: Default: no approval (dangerously-allow-all is now default). Custom approval policies possible via Plugin API (tool.call event).

sandbox_mode.comment: No built-in sandbox. Documentation suggests external isolated dev environments for untrusted code.

resume.comment: Full support. 'amp threads continue [thread id]' documented. Thread referencing (@T-xxx or URL) across sessions.

continue.comment: Full support. /continue command in CLI to pick old threads. Threads persist at ampcode.com/threads.

non_interactive.comment: Full support. amp --execute / amp -x for headless mode. Set AMP_API_KEY env var for CI/CD. Auto-detects piped stdout.

output_format.comment: Full support. --stream-json flag with --execute for Claude Code-compatible NDJSON streaming output. Multiple schema types.

statusline.comment: Not supported. Custom themes removed in the May 6, 2026 rebuild. No statusline customization.

telemetry.comment: No explicit telemetry settings found in documentation. No on/off telemetry toggle mentioned in manual or settings.

custom_model_provider.comment: No BYOM for the main agent (picks models per mode from providers). MCP servers can connect to external tools, and Plugin API allows custom tool registration.

notes: Amp is opinionated and aggressively removes features it considers obsolete (custom commands, slash commands, themes). Focuses on frontier models. Plugin API provides extensive extensibility for those who need it. Closed-source product; some auxiliary tools open source on GitHub.

pricing.comment: Usage-based: pay-as-you-go with zero markup on API costs for individuals. Minimum $5 credit purchase. Free mode (ad-supported, no longer shows ads). Enterprise: 50% markup + $1,000 one-time fee.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI runs locally. Threads shared via web but no managed agent platform.
